patch.value: pm package/extension init --capability schema: scaffold custom item type/field/migration starter

Complete the scaffold capability matrix toward the SDK's 9 capabilities by adding 'schema' — the keystone capability for universal domain customization. 'pm package init <name> --capability schema' (and the extension variant) generates a starter that registers a custom item type, a custom item field, and a schema migration via api.registerItemTypes/registerItemFields/registerMigration, plus the base ping command, with a runnable node:test suite using assertRegisteredItemType/assertRegisteredItemField/assertRegisteredMigration + runRegisteredMigrationForTest, and README define* guidance (defineItemType/defineItemField/defineMigration). KEY activation design: a schema starter that registers custom item TYPES must NOT declare narrow activation.commands — those are globally-scoped schema contributions that must be present for built-in commands (pm create <type>, pm list, pm validate) which the package does not own and cannot enumerate; with narrow activation.commands the custom type silently fails to register (empirically verified). The starter omits activation.commands so the conservative schema tier activates it for every command. Before schema, scaffold supported only commands/hooks/search/importers (4 of 9).

Added the 'schema' scaffold capability (5 of 9 SDK capabilities now scaffoldable). 'pm package init <name> --capability schema' and the extension variant generate a starter registering a custom item type, item field, and migration via api.registerItemTypes/registerItemFields/registerMigration plus the base ping command, with a runnable node:test suite (assertRegisteredItemType/Field/Migration + runRegisteredMigrationForTest) and README define* guidance (defineItemType/defineItemField/defineMigration). The schema starter deliberately omits manifest activation.commands (decision pm-halx) so the global custom type stays available to built-in commands via conservative activation. Drift-proofed shell completion by interpolating SCAFFOLD_CAPABILITIES. Docs updated (EXTENSIONS.md, SDK.md, help-content).

pm package/extension init --capability schema produces a type-checking, test-passing starter whose custom item type is usable via pm create <type>; 100% coverage; docs/completion/help updated; no regression to the other four capabilities.

Verified in a temp workspace: generated node:test 3/3 pass, tsc --noEmit exit 0, pm create sprint-tracker + alias sprinttracker + pm sprint tracker ping + package doctor (0 warnings) all succeed; manifest correctly omits activation.commands. Full suite 248 files / 5006 tests at 100/100/100/100 statements/branches/functions/lines; static-quality and docs-skills gates green. Follow-ups filed: pm-i5p5 (renderers/parser/preflight/services starters) and pm-ok47 (doctor warning for schema + narrow activation.commands).

The schema starter deliberately omits manifest activation.commands (decision pm-halx) so the global custom type stays available to built-in commands via conservative activation. Drift-proofed shell completion by interpolating SCAFFOLD_CAPABILITIES. Docs updated (EXTENSIONS.md, SDK.md, help-content).
